strategy_summary: The binary is a 64-bit ELF with NX enabled and no PIE. The vulnerable function reads up to 0x200 bytes into a 128-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use ROP to call system('/bin/sh') to get a shell. We need to find the offset to control the return address, and we already have the pop_rdi_ret gadget. We will also need the address of '/bin/sh' in the binary or libc.
